title: Agentic Coding in Xcode Implementation Guide (Part 2): Troubleshooting Checklist, Compatibility, and Team Collaboration
description: From individual experimentation to team reuse: A summary of frequently asked questions, version strategies, and collaboration guidelines for integrating Xcode + Gemini CLI into MCP.
content:
**TL;DR:** The upper limit of AI R&D assistants lies in constraints, not in the model itself. This endgame guide clarifies the troubleshooting and governance methodology for Xcode Agentic Coding for you and your team. It covers how to resolve MCP JSON data structure anomalies, avoid duplicate authorization pop-ups, and provides a ready-to-use [systematic management of code behavior boundaries](/posts/mastering-copilot-customization-cn/) specification library template to help enterprises smoothly enter the era of high-performance AI.

## Why You Need a "Troubleshooting Manual"

The most common pitfall in Agentic Coding isn't the prompt, but rather "environment and boundary conditions":

- Tool version mismatch

- MCP response format differences

- Inconsistent permission configurations

- Different usage habits among team members

This guide has a clear goal: to upgrade you from "occasionally usable" to "stable and reproducible."

![Agentic Coding Troubleshooting Guide](/assets/img/post/agentic-coding-03/ide_troubleshooting.png)

Series Navigation:

- [Part 1: From Concept to Runnable Environment](/posts/agentic-coding-xcode-gemini-01-overview/)

- [Part 2: Creating a Playable iOS Demo with a Prompt](/posts/agentic-coding-xcode-gemini-02-hands-on/)

- Part 3 (This Article): Troubleshooting Checklist, Compatibility, and Team Collaboration

## Frequently Asked Questions 1: Connected, but Tool Calls Abnormally

### Symptoms

-`/mcp list`The tool is visible

but the results after executing the tool are abnormal, or structured fields are missing

### Root Cause

In earlier versions of Xcode 26.3, there was a problem with the bridged responses.`content`Text-based JSON and`structuredContent`Inconsistent expectations.

![JSON data structure bug fix](/assets/img/post/agentic-coding-03/json_bug_fix.png)

### Handling

Ensure your Gemini CLI version is at least one that includes this compatibility fix.`>= 0.27.3`(More reliable).

```bash
gemini --version
npm install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest
```Reference repair path: Issue`#18371`与PR`#18376`(Link at the end of the article).

## Frequently Asked Question 2: Constantly prompting for permission confirmation creates a disjointed user experience.

### Recommendation

Enable MCP configuration by project; avoid keeping it enabled globally for extended periods.

You can keep separate configurations for each repository.`.gemini/settings.json`This way:

- Only the current project will request Xcode authorization

- Reduces noise from irrelevant projects

- Easier permission auditing

## Frequently Asked Question 3: Uncontrolled Agent Modification Scope

### Recommended Practice: Three-Stage Constraints

Clearly state the following in the task description:

1. **Allowed directories/files to be modified**

2. **Prohibited areas** (e.g., project configuration, signing, CI files)

3. **Each round must build before continuing**

Example:

```text
仅允许修改 Sources/UI 与 Sources/Scene。
不要修改工程签名、Targets 配置和 CI 文件。
每轮改动后都执行构建检查，失败先修复再做下一步。
```## Frequently Asked Question 4: "Everyone in the team can run it, but the results are inconsistent."

This is the most common collaboration problem. It is recommended to standardize the following four aspects:

![Team Collaboration and Rule Standardization](/assets/img/post/agentic-coding-03/team_collaboration.png)

### 1) Version Baseline

- Xcode version baseline (e.g., 26.3)

- Minimum Gemini CLI version (e.g., >= 0.27.3)

### 2) Session Standards

- Use a unified task template (goals/constraints/acceptance criteria)

- Include "success criteria" for each task

### 3) Code Review Rules

- Agent-generated code should also follow the same PR process

- Reject changes that are "committed without building"

### 4) Rollback Strategy

- Enable Git repositories

- Small-step commits, isolated by feature branches

## Recommended Team Task Template (Directly Reusable)

```text
[目标]
实现 XXX 功能，优先保证可构建与可运行。

[约束]
- 只修改 A/B/C 目录
- 不修改工程配置、签名与 CI
- 优先复用现有组件与命名规范

[执行策略]
1. 先给出最小实现
2. 构建并修复错误
3. 补齐测试或验证步骤
4. 输出变更摘要与风险点

[验收标准]
- 编译通过
- 关键交互可复现
- 无新增 warning（或说明原因）
```## Key Metrics from "Usable" to "Effective"

You can use these 3 metrics to measure the quality of Agentic Coding:

- **First-round pass rate**: The percentage of builds that compile successfully on the first attempt.

- **Fixed loop time**: The average time from error to successful build.

- **Reviewability**: Does the PR clearly demonstrate the intent and boundaries of the changes?

If all three are improving, it means you're not "playing with a new toy," but rather improving engineering efficiency.

## Series Conclusion

The core of these three articles can be summarized in one sentence:

> The upper limit of Agentic Coding does not depend on how smart the model is, but on how clear your engineering constraints are.

You can think of it as a new colleague:

- Give it a clear goal, and it will run fast.

- Give it vague requirements, and it will stray even further from the right path. (This statement is highly valuable.)
